description: O(n²) nested-loop deduplication replaced with O(n) map-based approach
response: [{"id":"H1","hunk_id":"hunk_f0_h0","question":"What is the worst-case time complexity of the original implementation vs the new one when processing n items?","options":["Both are O(n) — the map only changes memory layout, not asymptotic cost","Original is O(n²) due to the nested scan of seen; new is O(n) with amortized O(1) map lookups","Original is O(n log n) because the inner loop terminates early on average; new is O(n)","Both are O(n²) — Go maps degrade to O(n) lookup under hash collision"],"answer":1,"hint":"For each of the n items, how many comparisons does the original inner loop perform in the worst case?","explanation":"The original scans the entire seen slice for every input item, giving O(n) inner iterations across O(n) outer — O(n²) overall. The map-based version does O(1) average-case lookup and insert per item, reducing the total to O(n). The distinction matters for large or repeatedly-called inputs."}]
